byWKRC

LAWRENCEBURG, Ind. (WKRC) - Despite a lot of hometown support, Mason Parris' Olympic dreams have come to an end in Paris.

He lost his first round match in the early morning hours Friday to Mongolian wrestler, Lkhagvagerel Munkhtur.

If Munkhtur went to the final round, Parris would have had a shot at bronze, but Munkhtur lost his next match, so Parris has been eliminated.

A few dozen supporters woke up early for a watch party at Lawrenceburg Civic Park which began at 5 a.m.

(WKRC)

Mayor Kelly Mallaun said it was an honor to watch him compete on the world stage.

"That young man, outside being world class athlete is a world class young man."

2025Sinclair, Inc.
title: Dozens gather early to watch Lawrenceburg native wrestle Olympic match
description: Despite a lot of hometown support, Mason Parris' Olympic dreams have come to an end in Paris.
